Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Theme post: Shadow AI Is a Content Problem (Not a Governance Problem) — pre-tease for 6/17 blog
Description:
Topic: Pre-tease (T-1) for Travis-bylined Shadow AI POV publishing 2026-06-17 on cerkl.com. Counter-frame to Staffbase + LumApps + Simpplr ad creative (all framing shadow AI as a governance problem). Cerkl's wedge: it is a content problem. W1 of the 3-week wedge sprint (2026-06-17 → 2026-07-08). Replaces the previously planned webinar diagnostic poll (Rachel Folz webinar delayed).
Post type: static-theme
Wraps: shadow-ai-content-problem (source signal: ../content-plan/inputs.md §2026-06-03 and ../../research/ic-trends/team-update/2026-06-03.md §1)
Copy:
[CAPTION]

Three IC platforms have moved shadow AI from blog content to ad headlines this month. The stat being cited: 79% of organizations suspect employees are misusing AI tools they did not approve.

The pitch underneath those headlines, from every one of them, is governance. A control center. An approval workflow. A way to police what employees type into the tools IT did not pick.

The IC buyer does not sit in that conversation. IT does. CISO does. Legal does. The Director of Internal Communications is on a different call, trying to figure out why the new benefits policy did not land with the frontline.

Shadow AI is a real signal. The framing misreads what it is signalling.

Employees who reach for an unsanctioned AI to draft a customer email, a Slack post, a policy explainer have escaped a content gap. The sanctioned channel did not have the tool they needed, did not have the speed they needed, or did not trust them with either. Governance was downstream of that gap.

A better default fixes the gap. The AI-assisted send IC owns. Proof it landed. A read-rate the team can defend. Shadow AI loses its escape route the moment the sanctioned channel is the faster one.

Full POV tomorrow on the Cerkl blog.

[ASSET SPEC]

Visual concept: Counter-frame card. Left side, smaller and grayed: competitor framing as a pull-quote of the verbatim Staffbase / Simpplr / LumApps line ("Your employees are already using AI tools you haven't approved" or "Stop shadow AI without killing productivity"). Right side, larger and on-brand. The Cerkl reframe: "Shadow AI is a content problem dressed as a governance one."
Asset role: counter-frame card. Origination needed; no existing manifest. Pull rendered URL during asset-process step.
Brand direction: Cerkl brand colors per design CONTEXT. Logo lockup bottom-right. No headshot. Legible at LinkedIn mobile feed size.

[HASHTAGS]

#InternalCommunications #EmployeeEngagement #AIinIC

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Link post: How to Claim Your Strategic Seat in Internal Communications (Wed amplification)
Description:
Topic: Wed amplification link post for the Strategic Seat blog (publishes Mon 2026-06-15). Identity-crisis angle (AI eats the tactical layer; leadership has not yet pulled IC into the strategic conversation). Pairs with the Thu 2026-06-18 link post (T007) which carries the playbook angle (5 decisions + 4 conversations + executive vocabulary). Both push the same blog with different hooks. Replaces the previously planned Rachel Folz webinar registration push (webinar delayed).
Post type: static-blog
Wraps: claim-strategic-seat-internal-communications
Copy:
[CAPTION]

The IC identity crisis of 2026: AI moved into the tactical work IC used to own (the drafting, the formatting, the segmentation). Leadership has not, yet, moved IC into the conversations where the work IC should now own gets decided.

That gap is what "be more strategic" points at without saying.

The Cerkl blog from Monday names the five decisions IC managers and directors should be in the room for in 2026. The four conversations to start having now to get pulled into those rooms. And the executive vocabulary to use once you are there.

For practitioners caught between a tactical layer that is shrinking and a strategic layer that is still gatekept, the piece is a working playbook. The language. The metrics. The specific moves.

Read the full breakdown:

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Link post: How to Claim Your Strategic Seat in Internal Communications
Description:
Topic: Link post pointing to the strategic-seat blog
Post type: static-blog
Wraps: claim-strategic-seat-internal-communications
Copy:
[CAPTION]

"Be more strategic" is the most useless advice an IC manager gets from their CHRO. It names the thing without telling you how.

There are five decisions IC should be in the room for in 2026: AI rollout and policy, change communications for M&A or restructuring, crisis comms, the employee value proposition, and cross-functional measurement of engagement and retention signals. Each one has a version where IC is consulted late, and a version where IC is the architect.

Four specific conversations move you from the first version to the second. The new blog walks through the language to use, the metrics to bring, and the executive-vocabulary upgrade that gets IC pulled into the strategy meeting earlier.

For IC managers and directors caught between AI taking the tactical work and not being in the leadership conversation yet, this is a practitioner's playbook, not a pep talk.

Full breakdown:

https://cerkl.com/blog/claim-strategic-seat-internal-communications
